items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: Carragher warns Slot: No UCL, no 'leg to stand on'
items.summary: Former Liverpool defender Jamie Carragher believes head coach Arne Slot would not have "a leg to stand on" if they do not qualify for the Champions League.

items.title: PSG complete signing of Barça teen Fernández
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain have completed the signing of Barcelona's talented teenager Dro Fernández after exercising his €8 million ($9.5 million) release clause for the LaLiga side.

items.title: Voormalig Ajax-talent Silvano Vos in beeld bij Serie B-club
items.summary: Silvano Vos kan mogelijk aan de slag in de Serie B. De voormalige middenvelder van Ajax is in beeld bij US Avellino, dat hem wil huren van AC Milan. Dat bevestigen ingewijden.

items.title: Sneijder miste basisdebuut zoon Jessey in KKD: 'Was er niet, maar ook weer wel'
items.summary: Wesley Sneijder kon maandagavond helaas niet aanwezig zijn bij het basisdebuut van zijn zoon Jessey Sneijder voor Jong FC Utrecht. De 134-voudig international van Oranje was in de Verenigde Staten bij zijn andere zoon. Uiteraard hebben Wesley en Jessey wel online contact gehad.

items.title: Champions League permutations: who needs what from final night of fixtures?
items.summary: A staggering 32 of 36 teams go into the final set of matches with their hopes of staying in the competition still alive With seven wins from seven, Arsenal have a perfect record in the league phase. Only Bayern Munich and Inter have found the net against Mikel Arteta’s team, who dismissed Atlético Madrid 4-0 in October. The bottom side, Kairat, visit the Emirates Stadium on the final night, with Arsenal needing a draw to confirm top spot and, theoretically, the most favourable last-16 draw. Continue reading...

items.title: Aiyawatt Srivaddhanaprabha: ‘It’s love and pain. Leicester is like my son, so I have to do it right’
items.summary: The Leicester City chair plays down talk of another relegation but knows the mood among fans is fraught Leicester City are hurting but Aiyawatt “Top” Srivaddhanaprabha, looking towards the pitch at the King Power Stadium, insists he shares supporters’ frustrations. He acknowledges the warm glow of their extraordinary Premier League title win almost a decade ago has long faded. He watches every game, which sometimes means tuning in from Thailand in the early hours. An 8pm kick-off in England is a 3am start in Bangkok. “I want to see the real passion of the players and the performance,” the chair says. “When it is not there, I can’t sleep, so it’s love and pain. Leicester is like my son. So I have to do it right. Of course, a son can be naughty, a son can fail the exam, a pain in your head. The son can be top of the class, graduate, have a bad girlfriend or good wife, you never know. So I feel the same, but the love is there. The responsibility is there. The first thing for me is to identify the problem and fix it.” Continue reading...

items.title: 'Worrying' PSR, Rudkin's role and never walking away - Leicester owner speaks out
items.summary: Leicester City’s owner has told Sky Sports News that, as well as a new manager, he is looking for a chief executive, commercial director and sporting director, as part of a major overhaul of the club’s structure.

items.title: Bove: One airport meeting took me to Watford instead of the Premier League
items.summary: Former Roma midfielder Edoardo Bove has revealed how a chance meeting at an airport with Watford sporting director Gianluca Nani led him to signing for the club - despite being in "advanced talks" with a number of Premier League sides.

items.title: Dave Kwakman over zijn keuze voor FC Volendam en de geruchtmakende looptest bij AZ
items.summary: Zijn eerste twee wedstrijden bij FC Volendam sloot hij winnend af, maar drie op een rij was Dave Kwakman niet gegeven. Na de 2-0 nederlaag bij Ajax vertelde de middenvelder over zijn stap naar FC Volendam, de druk die deze keuze met zich meebrengt en de veelbesproken looptest die hem een week langer dan verwacht in Alkmaar hield.

items.title: Rijkhoff gniffelt na prachtgoal: 'Keepers, blijf lekker ver uit je goal spelen'
items.summary: Julian Rijkhoff genoot van zijn doelpunt in de wedstrijd tussen Jong AZ en Almere City . De Ajax-huurling schoot de bal van grote afstand over de Alkmaarse doelman Jur Schipper heen, waarmee hij bijdroeg aan de zege van Almere City.

items.title: Australian Open: Order of Play for Wednesday with Swiatek, Djokovic, Sinner in action
items.summary: Order of Play for Day 11 at the Australian Open.

How 'Premier League' Galatasaray can derail Man City
items.summary: Needing a positive result to keep their Champions League campaign on track, Galatasaray are not the side Manchester City would choose to face in their final game of the league phase.

items.title: Lessons from Man United's victory at Arsenal; Real Madrid win ugly; Liverpool woe; more
items.summary: Man United look better post-Ruben Amorim. Can they keep it going? And how quickly can Arsenal move past Sunday's loss? Gab Marcotti recaps the weekend.

items.title: Farioli kreeg kippenvel van zijn routinier: 'Boodschap was erg krachtig'
items.summary: In zijn Ajax-periode stond Francesco Farioli erom bekend dat hij zijn ervaren spelers toespraken liet houden in de kleedkamer, dit heeft de Italiaan voortgezet bij FC Porto. De 29-jarige Stephen Eustáquio was aan de beurt rondom de gewonnen wedstrijd tegen Gil Vicente en hij bezorgde Farioli kippenvel.

items.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
items.summary: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

items.title: Barry goal rescues draw for Everton against Leeds
items.summary: Watch Premier League highlights as Everton's Thierno Barry continues his good run of form with a second-half goal to earn the Toffees a 1-1 draw with Leeds United.

items.title: Bayern Munich open talks to extend Kane contract
items.summary: Bayern Munich sporting director Max Eberl says the German club are in talks with Harry Kane about extending the striker's contract.
